description_long: This object is a rectangular brown planter box filled with soil and containing two distinct types of artificial flowers. One group of flowers is yellow with large, rounded petals and prominent central discs, while the other group features purple flowers with more angular petals and yellow centers. The yellow flowers are taller and clustered together, while the purple flowers are shorter and spread out across the planter. Several green stems are visible, supporting the flowers and emerging from the soil. The planter itself has a slightly tapered shape, wider at the top and narrowing toward the base, with a smooth, matte finish. The overall appearance suggests a decorative flower arrangement suitable for urban outdoor or indoor settings.
description: A brown rectangular planter box holds tall yellow and shorter purple artificial flowers with green stems, arranged in a decorative cluster.
description_view_0: From this view, the planter's narrow side is visible, showing mostly the purple flowers and a few yellow flower stems behind them.
description_view_1: This view highlights the wide side of the planter, displaying both the tall yellow flowers and the spread of purple flowers clearly.
description_view_2: Here, the opposite narrow side is shown, with purple flowers in the foreground and yellow flower stems visible behind.
description_view_3: This view presents the wide side from the back, emphasizing the yellow flowers and showing the purple flowers lower and partially obscured.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with each flower displaying a whorled pattern around a central disc. The yellow flowers have overlapping, rounded petals, while the purple flowers have angular petals radiating symmetrically.
